bullets.text: The World Health Organization has declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo an international emergency, with nearly 250 suspected cases and 80 deaths reported.

Russia claims a large-scale Ukrainian drone attack killed three people in the Moscow region, with Ukraine's president stating the strikes are a justified response to Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.
Donald Trump announced that US and Nigerian forces killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, identified as Islamic State's 'second in command' and 'most active terrorist in the world,' in a complex mission.
The newly merged Adelaide University is facing protests from students over its decision to change the venue for graduation ceremonies from Bonython Hall to Pridham Hall.
Australia's two largest supermarket chains, Coles and Woolworths, have been found to switch promotions on various products almost simultaneously, raising concerns about market practices.
Support for the Labor government and Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has declined following the recent federal budget, according to new polling data.
The looming energy crisis in Iran is intensifying, with emergency measures spreading as oil stockpiles run low, signaling a supply squeeze that will worsen before improving.
Investors are concerned about rising global interest rates and the threat of inflation, as there is no clear end in sight to the Iran war, which is a key economic consequence.
Economists are observing a deepening mystery in commodity markets as the world has managed to avoid an oil catastrophe despite ongoing geopolitical tensions.
